rules: When asked for game detail columns like game_pk, winner, game_date — do NOT use COUNT(*) or GROUP BY. Return one row per game.
When asked 'how many games' AND detail columns — return the detail rows. The count is implied by the number of rows.
NEVER join dw.teams with both home and away conditions on the same alias. Always use two separate joins: t_home and t_away.
To filter for games between two specific teams, use: WHERE ((t_home.name = 'Team A' AND t_away.name = 'Team B') OR (t_home.name = 'Team B' AND t_away.name = 'Team A'))
To determine winner using scores: CASE WHEN g.teams__home__score > g.teams__away__score THEN t_home.name ELSE t_away.name END AS winner
To determine winner using is_winner flag: CASE WHEN g.teams__home__is_winner = 1 THEN t_home.name ELSE t_away.name END AS winner
For NL West division games both teams must have: t_home.division__name = 'National League West' AND t_away.division__name = 'National League West'
For games involving at least one NL team use: (t_home.league__name = 'National League' OR t_away.league__name = 'National League')
Always filter completed games with: g.status__detailed_state = 'Final'
For regular season only add: g.series_description = 'Regular Season'
Check the table for season or years when question has a year or year range
common_mistakes: Using COUNT(*) with GROUP BY when the question asks for per-game detail rows
Joining dw.teams once with AND on both home and away team IDs — this returns zero rows
Missing the team name filter when the question specifies two teams
Using a specific date like '2025-06-30' when the question asks about a full year
Using OR instead of AND when filtering for division games — both teams must be in the same division
Joining on __link columns instead of __id columns
